phren - Long-term memory for your AI agents.
npm package: @phren/cli

Quick start:
  phren init
  This creates ~/.phren, registers MCP for detected agents, sets up lifecycle hooks, and registers your machine.
  Open a prompt in your project directory. Context injection starts immediately.

MCP Tools (67 across 12 modules):

  Search and browse:
    search_knowledge(query, type?, limit?, project?, tag?, since?, status?, include_history?, synthesize?) - FTS5 search with synonym expansion, filterable by tag/date/lifecycle
    get_memory_detail(id) - fetch full content of a memory by id (e.g. mem:project/filename)
    get_project_summary(name) - project summary card and file list
    list_projects(page?, page_size?) - all projects in active profile with pagination
    get_findings(project, limit?, include_superseded?, include_history?, status?) - read recent findings without a search query

  Task management:
    get_tasks(project?, id?, item?, status?, limit?, done_limit?, offset?, summary?) - read tasks with pagination, summary mode, and section filtering
    add_task(project, item: string | string[]) - add one or more tasks to Queue section
    complete_task(project, item: string | string[]) - match by text, move to Done
    remove_task(project, item: string | string[]) - remove one or more tasks by matching text
    update_task(project, item, updates) - change text, priority, context, section, or linked GitHub issue
    link_task_issue(project, item, issue_number?, issue_url?, unlink?) - link or unlink an existing GitHub issue on a task item
    promote_task_to_issue(project, item, repo?, title?, body?, mark_done?) - create a GitHub issue from a task item and link it back
    pin_task(project, item) - pin a task so it stays visible across sessions
    work_next_task(project) - pick the next highest-priority task to work on
    promote_task(project, item, move_to_active?) - promote a task to a higher priority section
    tidy_done_tasks(project, keep?, dry_run?) - archive completed tasks to keep the list clean

  Finding capture:
    add_finding(project, finding: string | string[], citation?, findingType?, sessionId?, source?) - append one or more findings; citation/type/provenance apply to single-string mode
      source: "human" | "agent" | "hook" | "extract" | "consolidation" | "unknown"
      findingType: "decision" | "pitfall" | "pattern" | "tradeoff" | "architecture" | "bug"
    supersede_finding(project, finding_text, superseded_by) - mark a finding as superseded
    retract_finding(project, finding_text, reason) - retract a finding with reason metadata
    resolve_contradiction(project, finding_text, finding_text_other, resolution) - resolve contradiction lifecycle state
    get_contradictions(project?, finding_text?) - list unresolved contradicted findings
    edit_finding(project, old_text, new_text) - edit a finding in place while preserving inline metadata
    remove_finding(project, finding: string | string[]) - remove one or more findings by matching text
    push_changes(message?) - commit and push all phren changes

  Fragment graph:
    search_fragments(query, project?, limit?) - find fragments and their related docs
    get_related_docs(entity, project?, limit?) - get docs linked to a named fragment
    read_graph(project?, limit?, offset?) - read the fragment graph with pagination
    link_findings(project, finding_text, entity, relation?, entity_type?) - manually link a finding to a fragment
    cross_project_fragments(entity, exclude_project?, limit?) - find fragments shared across multiple projects

  Session management (for agents without hooks):
    session_start(project?, connectionId?) - mark session start, retrieve prior session context + checkpoints, return sessionId
    session_end(summary?, sessionId?, connectionId?) - end a session, save summary, write task checkpoint/impact outcomes
    session_context(sessionId?, connectionId?) - get current session state by explicit identity
    session_history(limit?, sessionId?, project?) - list past sessions or drill into a specific session

  Memory quality:
    pin_memory(project, memory) - write a truth into truths.md that bypasses decay
    memory_feedback(key, feedback) - record helpful/reprompt/regression outcomes

  Data management:
    add_project(path, profile?, ownership?) - bootstrap a repo or working directory into phren and add it to the active profile
    export_project(project) - export project data as portable JSON
    import_project(data) - import project from exported JSON
    manage_project(project, action) - archive or unarchive a project

  Skills management:
    list_skills(project?) - list all installed skills with metadata
    read_skill(name, project?) - read full skill file content and parsed frontmatter
    write_skill(name, content, scope) - create or update a skill (scope: 'global' or project name)
    remove_skill(name, project?) - delete a skill file
    enable_skill(name, project) - enable a skill without deleting its file
    disable_skill(name, project) - disable a skill without deleting its file
    resolution model:
      precedence: project scope overrides global scope
      alias collisions: conflicting commands/aliases are marked unregistered
      visibility gating: disabled skills stay on disk but are hidden from active mirrors
      generated artifacts: .claude/skill-manifest.json + .claude/skill-commands.json

  Hooks management:
    list_hooks(project?) - show hook status for all tools + custom hooks + config paths, optionally including project overrides
    toggle_hooks(enabled, tool?, project?, event?) - enable/disable hooks globally, per tool, or per tracked project/event
    add_custom_hook(event, command?, webhook?, secret?, timeout?) - add a custom integration hook (command or webhook)
    remove_custom_hook(event, command?) - remove custom hooks by event/command match

  Health and review:
    get_consolidation_status(project?) - check if findings need consolidation
    health_check() - run doctor checks and return results
    list_hook_errors(limit?) - show recent hook errors and failures
    get_review_queue(project?) - read items waiting for review (review queue is read-only)
    doctor_fix(check_data?) - run doctor self-heal checks and apply fixes automatically

  Finding extraction:
    auto_extract_findings(project, text, model?, dryRun?) - extract findings from text (max 10000 chars)

  Configuration:
    get_config(domain?) - read governance and policy config (proactivity, taskMode, findingSensitivity, retention, workflow, access, index, or all)
    set_proactivity(level, scope?) - set agent proactivity level (high/medium/low) with optional scope (base/findings/tasks)
    set_task_mode(mode) - set task management mode (off, manual, suggest, auto)
    set_finding_sensitivity(level) - set finding capture sensitivity (minimal, conservative, balanced, aggressive)
    set_retention_policy(settings) - configure retention and decay policy
    set_workflow_policy(settings) - configure workflow approval gates
    set_index_policy(settings) - configure indexer include/exclude globs

CLI Commands:

  Interactive:
    phren                               - interactive shell (TTY default)
    phren shell                         - explicit shell mode
    phren quickstart                    - quick setup: init + project scaffold

  Search and discovery:
    phren search <query> [--project <n>] [--type <t>] [--limit <n>] - FTS5 search
    phren search --history              - show recent searches
    phren search --from-history <n>     - re-run search #n
    phren search-fragments <query>      - search fragments (libraries/tools/concepts)
    phren related-docs <entity>         - find docs linked to a fragment
    phren graph [--project <n>] [--limit <n>] - show fragment knowledge graph
    phren graph link <project> "finding" "fragment" - link finding to fragment

  Finding management:
    phren add-finding <project> "..."   - append a finding
    phren pin <project> "..."           - pin a truth (canonical memory)
    phren review [project]              - show review queue items
    phren consolidation-status [project]- check if findings need consolidation
    phren finding add <project> "<text>"         - add finding
    phren finding remove <project> "<text>"      - remove finding
    phren finding list <project>                 - list findings
    phren finding supersede <project> "<text>" --by "<newer>" - mark superseded
    phren finding retract <project> "<text>" --reason "<reason>" - retract
    phren finding contradictions [project]       - list contradicted findings
    phren finding resolve <project> "<a>" "<b>" <keep_a|keep_b|keep_both|retract_both> - resolve contradiction

  Task management:
    phren tasks                         - cross-project task view
    phren task add <project> "<text>"   - add task
    phren task complete <project> "<text>" - move task to done
    phren task remove <project> "<text>"  - remove task
    phren task next <project>           - pick highest-priority task
    phren task promote <project> "<text>" [--active] - promote priority
    phren task tidy <project> [--keep=<n>] [--dry-run] - archive completed
    phren task link <project> "<text>" --issue <number> - link to GitHub issue
    phren task link <project> "<text>" --unlink  - remove GitHub link
    phren task create-issue <project> "<text>" [--repo <owner/name>] [--done] - create GitHub issue from task
    phren task update <project> "<text>" [--priority=high|medium|low] [--section=Active|Queue|Done] - update metadata
    phren task pin <project> "<text>"   - pin task to stay visible

  Skills management:
    phren skills list [--project <name>]  - list installed skills
    phren skills show <name>              - show skill content
    phren skills edit <name>              - edit skill in $EDITOR
    phren skills add <project> <path>     - link/copy skill into project
    phren skills enable <project|global> <name>  - enable disabled skill
    phren skills disable <project|global> <name> - disable skill
    phren skills remove <project> <name>  - remove project skill
    phren skills resolve <project|global> - print resolved skill manifest
    phren skills doctor <project|global>  - diagnose skill visibility
    phren skills sync <project|global>    - regenerate resolved mirror
    phren skill-list                      - alias for skills list
    phren detect-skills [--import]        - find untracked skills

  Hooks management:
    phren hooks list [--project <name>]   - show hook status
    phren hooks show <tool>               - show hook config
    phren hooks edit <tool>               - edit hook config
    phren hooks enable <tool>             - enable hooks for tool
    phren hooks disable <tool>            - disable hooks for tool
    phren hooks add-custom <event> <command> - add custom hook
    phren hooks remove-custom <event> [<command>] - remove custom hooks
    phren hooks errors [--limit <n>]      - show recent hook errors

  Project management:
    phren add [path] [--ownership <mode>] - add directory as project
    phren projects list                   - list all projects
    phren projects configure <name> [--ownership <mode>] [--hooks on|off] - update settings
    phren projects remove <name>          - remove project
    phren projects export <name>          - export project as JSON
    phren projects import <data>          - import project from JSON
    phren projects archive <name>         - archive project
    phren projects unarchive <name>       - restore archived project

  Configuration:
    phren config policy get|set ...       - retention, TTL, decay, confidence
    phren config workflow get|set ...     - approval workflow settings
    phren config access get|set ...       - role-based permissions
    phren config index get|set ...        - indexer include/exclude globs
    phren config synonyms [list|add|remove] - manage learned synonyms
    phren config project-ownership [mode] - default ownership for new projects
    phren config telemetry [on|off]       - opt-in usage telemetry
    phren config machines                 - registered machines
    phren config profiles                 - profiles and projects

  Maintenance:
    phren maintain govern [project]       - queue stale/conflicting memories
    phren maintain prune [project]        - delete expired entries
    phren maintain consolidate [project]  - deduplicate FINDINGS.md
    phren maintain extract [project]      - mine git and GitHub signals

  Debugging and status:
    phren status                          - health, active project, stats
    phren doctor [--fix] [--check-data] [--agents] [--parity] - health check
    phren debug-injection --prompt "..."  - preview hook-prompt injection
    phren inspect-index [--project <n>]   - inspect FTS index contents
    phren sessions [session-id]           - session history; drill into session
    phren session-context                 - show current session state
    phren quality-feedback --key=<k> --type=helpful|reprompt|regression - record memory feedback
    phren web-ui [--port=3499] [--no-open]- lightweight memory web UI
    phren verify                          - post-init verification
    phren update [--refresh-starter]      - update package
    phren uninstall                       - remove phren config and hooks

  Modes:
    phren mcp-mode on|off|status          - toggle MCP integration
    phren hooks-mode on|off|status        - toggle hook execution

Environment Variables:

  PHREN_PATH                    - override phren directory (default: ~/.phren)
  PHREN_PROFILE                 - active profile name (filters indexed projects)
  PHREN_ACTOR                   - identity for access control and audit logging
  PHREN_DEBUG                   - set to 1 for debug logging to ~/.phren/.runtime/debug.log
  PHREN_CONTEXT_TOKEN_BUDGET    - max approx tokens injected by hook-prompt (default: 550)
  PHREN_CONTEXT_SNIPPET_LINES   - max lines per injected snippet (default: 6)
  PHREN_CONTEXT_SNIPPET_CHARS   - max chars per injected snippet (default: 520)
  PHREN_FEATURE_AUTO_EXTRACT    - toggle git/GitHub mining in hook-prompt (default: on, set 0 to disable)
  PHREN_FEATURE_DAILY_MAINTENANCE - toggle detached daily governance (default: on, set 0 to disable)
  PHREN_FINDINGS_CAP            - max active findings before auto-archiving (default: 20)
  PHREN_LLM_ENDPOINT            - OpenAI-compatible endpoint for semantic dedup/conflict
  PHREN_LLM_KEY                 - API key for LLM endpoint (or OPENAI_API_KEY)
  PHREN_LLM_MODEL               - model name for LLM endpoint
  PHREN_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER      - "api" or unset; enables semantic search fallback
  PHREN_EMBEDDING_API_URL       - OpenAI-compatible /embeddings endpoint (e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1)
  PHREN_EMBEDDING_API_KEY       - Bearer token for PHREN_EMBEDDING_API_URL
  PHREN_FEATURE_SEMANTIC_DEDUP  - set 1 to enable LLM-based paraphrase dedup
  PHREN_FEATURE_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT - set 1 to enable LLM-based contradiction detection
  PHREN_FEATURE_AUTO_CAPTURE    - set 1 to extract insights from conversation transcripts at session end
  PHREN_FEATURE_HYBRID_SEARCH   - TF-IDF cosine fallback (default: on, set 0 to disable)
  PHREN_FEATURE_PROGRESSIVE_DISCLOSURE - compact memory index injection (set 1 to enable)
  PHREN_FEATURE_GIT_CONTEXT_FILTER - git-context file relevance boost (set 'true' to enable)
  PHREN_MAX_INJECT_TOKENS       - hard cap on total injected tokens (default: 2000)
  PHREN_TASK_PRIORITY            - priorities to inject: high,medium,low (default: high,medium)
  PHREN_MEMORY_TTL_DAYS          - override memory TTL for trust filtering
  PHREN_OLLAMA_URL               - Ollama base URL (default: http://localhost:11434, set 'off' to disable)
  PHREN_EMBEDDING_MODEL          - embedding model (default: nomic-embed-text)
  PHREN_EXTRACT_MODEL            - Ollama model for extraction (default: llama3.2)
  PHREN_CONSOLIDATION_CAP        - max total findings before forced consolidation (default: 150)
  PHREN_HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS          - timeout for hook subprocess execution (default: 14000)
  PHREN_SLOW_FS_WARN_MS          - warn when FS operations exceed threshold (default: 3000)
  PHREN_FILE_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS    - max file lock wait time (default: 5000)
  PHREN_AUTOCAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS  - min ms between auto-capture runs (default: 30000)
  PHREN_AUTOCAPTURE_SESSION_CAP  - max findings auto-captured per session (default: 10)
  PHREN_GH_PR_LIMIT              - max PRs fetched per extraction (default: 40)
  PHREN_GH_TIMEOUT_MS            - GitHub API timeout (default: 10000)
  PHREN_MEMORY_EXTRACT_WINDOW_DAYS - look-back window for extraction (default: 30)

Key File Locations:

  ~/.phren/                          - phren root directory
  ~/.phren/machines.yaml             - hostname-to-profile mapping
  ~/.phren/profiles/                 - profile YAML files listing projects per profile
  ~/.phren/<project>/summary.md      - five-line project summary card
  ~/.phren/<project>/CLAUDE.md       - full project context (architecture, commands, conventions)
  ~/.phren/<project>/topic-config.json - project-owned archive topic definitions
  ~/.phren/<project>/reference/      - deep reference docs and archived topic buckets
  ~/.phren/<project>/reference/topics/ - machine-managed topic docs for archived findings
  ~/.phren/<project>/FINDINGS.md     - bugs, patterns, decisions, things to avoid
  ~/.phren/<project>/tasks.md        - task queue across sessions
  ~/.phren/<project>/review.md               - items pending human review
  ~/.phren/.runtime/                 - audit.log, search history, caches
  ~/.phren/.runtime/access-control.local.json - local RBAC role augmentation
  ~/.phren/.runtime/telemetry.json   - opt-in local telemetry counters
  ~/.phren/.sessions/                - task checkpoint files for cross-session resume
  ~/.phren/.governance/              - governance policy JSON files
  ~/.phren/global/skills/            - personal workflow slash commands
  ~/.phren/global/CLAUDE.md          - user-level agent instructions

Finding Tags:

  Findings can be tagged with one of eleven types:
    [decision]      architectural choice with rationale — never decays
    [pattern]       reusable approach that works well
    [pitfall]       bug or failure mode to avoid
    [observation]   time-bounded note — expires in 14 days
    [bug]           confirmed defect or failure
    [workaround]    temporary fix or hack
    [tooling]       tool or dependency note
    [context]       situational note about current state
    [anti-pattern]  approach to avoid and why
    [tradeoff]      deliberate compromise with rationale
    [architecture]  structural design note
  Use findingType param in add_finding, or prefix manually: "- [pitfall] ..."
  Tags are filterable in search_knowledge via the tag parameter.
  Auto-tagging: findings without an explicit tag are auto-detected from content
    ("We decided..." → [decision], "Watch out for..." → [pitfall], etc.).

Retrieval Behavior:

  Lifecycle penalties: superseded findings 0.25×, retracted 0.1×, contradicted 0.4× confidence
  Inactive findings (superseded/retracted) are stripped from the FTS index entirely
  Decay resistance: confirmed findings decay 3× slower when repeatedly useful
  Session context diff: session_start reports count of new findings since last session
  Snippet dedup: findings appearing in both project and global files are injected once
  Session momentum: hot topics get up to 30% more token budget as work progresses
  Auto-tagging: findings without type tags are inferred from content at write time
  Fragment expansion: related fragment docs are included when a finding links to a fragment

Agent Support:

  Claude Code    - full lifecycle hooks (SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop) + MCP
  Cursor         - MCP + generated hooks config + session wrapper
  VS Code Copilot- MCP integration (workspace or user depending mode)
  Copilot CLI    - MCP + generated hooks config + session wrapper
  Codex CLI      - MCP + generated hooks config + session wrapper
  Windsurf       - manual MCP setup (see docs/ide-setup.md)

Security and governance notes:
  Web UI security: loopback-only bind, per-run auth token, CSRF on mutations, CSP headers
  RBAC sources: .governance/access-control.json + .runtime/access-control.local.json
  Telemetry: opt-in only (`phren config telemetry on`), local storage only, no external reporting by default

Docs: https://alaarab.github.io/phren/
Source: https://github.com/alaarab/phren
